title: centaurium littorale var. glomeratum
intro: Centaurium littorale var. glomeratum, commonly known as Shore Gentian or Grass-of-Parnassus, is a rare, perennial herb native to Europe, Asia, and parts of North Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. It is believed to offer various health benefits, such as soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and supporting the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tincture, infusion, or decoction, and is used in various herbal preparations to leverage its medicinal properties.
